obligations.reason: The fact layer shows SimJTAG, dmi_jtag, SimDTM, jtag_req/dmi_req/debug_req signals, jtag_key, and muxing by jtag_enable. Need to verify whether the visible RTL mediates external debug access before it can affect protected state or core control.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester
obligations.object: debug capability and any protected registers/memory reachable through debug_req/debug_resp
obligations.expected_guard: debug/JTAG/DMI requests that can access protected SoC state or control core reset/halt must be covered by an explicit debug authorization/authentication or enable policy, not only by a mux selecting JTAG vs DMI
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_testharness debug selection: SimJTAG/dmi_jtag/SimDTM -> debug_req/debug_resp -> downstream debug module/bus effects
state_conditions_to_check: whether dmi_req_valid is tied off for non-JTAG path
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, expected guard, observed path
obligations.uncertainty: The visible scope may only be a test harness; downstream debug module details may be outside scope.

obligations.reason: Signals ndmreset, ndmreset_n, debug_req_core, and debug request muxing are present; check whether debug control operations are mediated before affecting reset/debug state.
obligations.subject: external debug/JTAG/DMI interface
obligations.expected_guard: debug-originated reset, halt, or core debug request should require debug authorization and should not be triggerable solely from unauthenticated JTAG/DMI traffic
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_testharness: ndmreset/debug_req_core/debug request signals into SoC/core reset and debug control
obligations.uncertainty: Need downstream instance connections to determine whether these signals reach protected core control.

obligations.reason: The wrapper has priv_lvl_i, access_ctrl_i, start/end address inputs, s_valid_rule='1, and connectivity_map_o computed from access_ctrl_i. Need to verify routing cannot bypass privilege/connectivity policy.
obligations.subject: AXI bus manager/requester
obligations.object: SoC address-map subordinates including AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, CLINT/PLIC, DRAM
obligations.expected_guard: AXI manager-to-subordinate connectivity must be gated by the correct access_ctrl entry indexed by current privilege level for every protected subordinate, with no unintended aliases or unconditional valid rules bypassing the map
obligations.candidate_path: axi_node_intf_wrap/connectivity_mapping: access_ctrl_i + priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_map_o -> AXI routing rules
obligations.uncertainty: The actual axi_node instance and policy update mechanism may be partially outside the provided facts.

obligations.reason: The explicit expression grants j==6 connectivity if access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i] is set. Since package enumerates PLIC=6 and CLINT=7, verify this alias does not unintentionally expand permissions.
obligations.subject: AXI requester indexed by i
obligations.object: PLIC subordinate index 6 and CLINT subordinate index 7 connectivity policy
obligations.expected_guard: special-case permission aliasing between subordinate 7 (CLINT) and subordinate 6 (PLIC) must be intentional and must not grant access to PLIC when only CLINT is authorized, unless both objects share the same policy
obligations.candidate_path: connectivity_mapping special case: connectivity_map_o[i][j] includes ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i])
required_evidence_slots: access_ctrl policy entries for indices 6 and 7
knowledge_used: Common false-positive risks: explain expected guard
obligations.uncertainty: Need confirm that package indices are the same indices consumed by connectivity_mapping in this instantiation.

obligations.reason: The wrapper passes start/end address arrays and sets s_valid_rule to all ones. Check whether all visible address ranges are valid, non-overlapping, and privilege/connectivity is applied after decode.
obligations.subject: AXI manager/requester
obligations.operation: decode and route AXI AW/AR transactions
obligations.object: address-decoded protected regions (DebugBase, ROMBase, ROM2Base, AESBase, CLINTBase, PLICBase, DRAMBase)
obligations.expected_guard: address range checks should reject out-of-range or overlapping protected targets and should apply consistently to both read and write address channels
obligations.candidate_path: axi_node_intf_wrap: start_addr_i/end_addr_i -> s_start_addr/s_end_addr; s_valid_rule='1; AXI AW/AR address routing
obligations.uncertainty: End addresses and axi_node internals may not be fully visible in the fact layer.

obligations.reason: The rom2 always block writes secure_reg indexed by addr_i when req_i and we_i are true, and facts show ROM2 address-map entry. Check whether any wrapper or bus guard prevents unauthorized writes to this protected ROM/secure storage.
obligations.subject: bus requester driving rom2 req_i/we_i
obligations.expected_guard: ROM2/secure_reg writes should be blocked or require an explicit privileged/authorized write guard; a ROM-like protected memory should not be writable merely when req_i && we_i
obligations.candidate_path: rom2: req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg array update; rdata_o readback
state_conditions_to_check: reset state loads mem into secure_reg
required_evidence_slots: requester and bus path to rom2
obligations.uncertainty: Whether ROM2 is intended immutable/protected must be inferred from module name and secure_reg; confirm with surrounding decode and policy.

obligations.reason: rdata_o continuously reflects secure_reg[raddr_q] for the last captured read address, independent of req_i in the assignment. Check whether external bus response valid/authorization prevents stale or unauthorized observation.
obligations.subject: bus requester observing rom2 rdata_o
obligations.expected_guard: reads of secure_reg/ROM2 should be gated by request validity and access authorization, and stale raddr_q data should not remain externally observable after unauthorized or idle cycles
obligations.candidate_path: rom2: raddr_q update only on req_i && !we_i; rdata_o = secure_reg[raddr_q]
state_conditions_to_check: idle cycles after a valid read
unauthorized requests blocked upstream but rdata_o still toggles
reset initializes secure_reg and raddr_q state
obligations.uncertainty: Need visible bus wrapper behavior to know whether rdata_o is sampled only under an authorized response.

obligations.reason: The read data path checks raddr_q < RomSize, but the write path assigns secure_reg[addr_i[...]] <= wdata_i without an obvious local bounds check. Verify decode/range guards prevent out-of-range writes or undefined effects.
obligations.subject: bus requester writing ROM2
obligations.object: secure_reg array and adjacent/undefined memory behavior
obligations.expected_guard: writes should either be range-checked against RomSize or the upstream address decoder must guarantee addr_i indexes are within secure_reg bounds
obligations.candidate_path: rom2: addr_i index into secure_reg write path without visible bounds check
state_conditions_to_check: bus range decode for ROM2 window
obligations.uncertainty: SystemVerilog array out-of-range semantics and synthesis behavior may depend on implementation; upstream decode may be sufficient.

obligations.reason: Facts show access_ctrl derived from access_ctrl_reg and used by connectivity mapping. The policy state is security-sensitive; verify where it is written/reset and whether updates are privileged and lock-protected.
obligations.subject: software/bus/debug requester configuring access_ctrl_reg
obligations.expected_guard: software-visible or externally driven access_ctrl_reg updates must themselves be privileged/locked and must not allow unprivileged code to change the access policy that protects subordinates
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_testharness: access_ctrl_reg -> access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node connectivity policy
state_conditions_to_check: lock bit or lifecycle state if present
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer excerpt shows use but not all write sites; inspect full testharness for declarations and assignments.

obligations.reason: The harness assigns a single priv_lvl from priv_lvl_processor, while the interconnect may have multiple managers/requesters. Check whether all subjects are correctly represented and whether privilege is latched per request.
obligations.subject: AXI requesters including processor, debug, or DMA-like masters
obligations.operation: authorize AXI read/write based on privilege
obligations.expected_guard: privilege level used for bus access control must correspond to the requesting transaction/master and be stable for the transaction, not a global value that can mismatch DMA/debug/other masters or change mid-transfer
state_conditions_to_check: privilege mode transition during outstanding transaction
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect instantiation parameters and master count to confirm whether multiple requester classes share this privilege signal.

obligations.reason: Facts show aw_prot/ar_prot/user signals being passed through the wrapper and DRAM bridge, while permission decisions may use separate priv_lvl/access_ctrl. Verify no protected target depends on missing or stale attributes.
obligations.subject: AXI requester with prot/user metadata
obligations.object: AXI security/privilege attributes and downstream access decisions
obligations.expected_guard: security attributes such as AXI prot/user must be preserved or explicitly checked; downstream protected targets should not rely on attributes that are dropped, overwritten, or inconsistent across channels
obligations.candidate_path: AXI metadata propagation: aw_prot/ar_prot/aw_user/ar_user through axi_node_intf_wrap and DRAM bridge
state_conditions_to_check: user fields for write data and response
obligations.uncertainty: Downstream target attribute checks may not be present in visible scope.

evidence_slots.subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester from SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM
evidence_slots.object: dm_top debug capability and downstream AXI/core control effects reachable through debug_req/debug_resp
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug/JTAG/DMI requests that can access protected SoC state or control core reset/halt must be covered by explicit authorization/authentication, not only a mux selecting JTAG vs DMI
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: debug_req_valid, debug_req, and debug_resp_ready are selected solely by jtag_enable[0] and connected to dm_top DMI ports. jtag_key is present and connected to dmi_jtag in the observed report, but no visible harness gate checks it before debug_req_valid/debug_req reaches dm_top. The non-JTAG SimDTM path is tied off only when InclSimDTM is false; the default parameter is InclSimDTM=1'b1.
evidence_slots.path: SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM -> jtag_dmi_req/dmi_req -> jtag_enable mux -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top.dmi_req_i/dmi_req_valid_i -> dm_top AXI/core debug effects
evidence_slots.state_condition: jtag_enable[0] selects path; init_done=rst_ni; debug_req_valid/debug_req_ready and debug_resp_valid/debug_resp_ready handshakes are passed to dm_top; dmi_req_valid is assigned '0 only in the non-InclSimDTM generate branch.
evidence_slots.impact: External or simulation debug traffic can reach the debug module interface and potentially access protected state or initiate control effects without a visible authorization gate in the harness.

rtl_evidence.detail: declares debug_req_valid/ready, debug_resp_valid/ready, jtag_req fields, dmi_req_valid, and jtag_key
debug_req_valid/debug_resp_ready/debug_req/exit_o and response valid signals are muxed only on jtag_enable[0]
dmi_req_valid/debug_req_bits_op/dmi_exit tied off in non-SimDTM branch
dm_top receives selected debug_req_valid/debug_req and returns debug_resp

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dm_top, dmi_jtag, and SimDTM internals are not visible; if authentication occurs inside those blocks, it is outside the supplied evidence.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Visible harness multiplexes external JTAG/SimDTM DMI traffic into dm_top based on jtag_enable without a visible authorization/authentication guard before debug requests reach dm_top.
inspection_records.notes: The candidate is scoped to the visible harness mediation obligation.

evidence_slots.subject: external debug/JTAG/DMI interface
evidence_slots.object: core reset/debug control state (ndmreset, ndmreset_n, debug_req_core)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: debug-originated reset, halt, or core debug request should require debug authorization and not be triggerable solely from unauthenticated JTAG/DMI traffic
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_top.ndmreset_o drives ndmreset, which is combined with rst_ni in rstgen to create ndmreset_n. dm_top.debug_req_o drives debug_req_core, which connects to ariane.debug_req_i. No visible authorization gate is shown between selected DMI traffic and dm_top control outputs.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG/SimDTM DMI traffic -> debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top -> ndmreset_o/debug_req_o -> rstgen and ariane.debug_req_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: rstgen input uses rst_ni & ~ndmreset; dm_top reset inputs use rst_ni; ariane reset uses ndmreset_n; jtag_enable selects the request source.
evidence_slots.impact: Debug-originated traffic may reset the SoC or force core debug entry through visible control outputs without a visible harness-level authorization policy.

rtl_evidence.detail: declares ndmreset, ndmreset_n, debug_req_core
rstgen input rst_ni is rst_ni & (~ndmreset), output rst_no is ndmreset_n
dm_top ndmreset_o connects to ndmreset and debug_req_o connects to debug_req_core
ariane.rst_ni is ndmreset_n and ariane.debug_req_i is debug_req_core

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: dm_top internal authorization behavior is not visible.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: dm_top outputs ndmreset and debug_req_core to reset/core debug controls, while the visible incoming debug path is gated only by the JTAG/DMI mux and handshakes.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate depends on downstream dm_top semantics but visible control connectivity is established.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI bus manager/requester
evidence_slots.object: SoC address-map subordinates including AES, ROM2, ROM, Debug, CLINT/PLIC, DRAM
evidence_slots.expected_guard: manager-to-subordinate connectivity must be gated by the correct access_ctrl entry indexed by current privilege for each protected subordinate, with no unintended aliases or unconditional valid-rule bypass
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: connectivity_map_o[i][j] is assigned access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] OR a j==6 special case using access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]. s_valid_rule is assigned '1. Harness passes a single priv_lvl derived from priv_lvl_processor to the xbar.
evidence_slots.path: access_ctrl_reg -> access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node_intf_wrap.access_ctrl_i + priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_mapping.connectivity_map_o -> axi_node cfg_connectivity_map_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: priv_lvl=priv_lvl_processor; access_ctrl[i][j]=access_ctrl_reg[i][4*j +: 4]; s_valid_rule='1; start/end addresses passed from ariane_soc package constants.
evidence_slots.impact: An alias or global privilege mismatch can broaden connectivity relative to the intended protected target policy.

rtl_evidence.detail: priv_lvl_processor assigned to priv_lvl; access_ctrl derived from access_ctrl_reg slices
axi_node_intf_wrap instantiated with priv_lvl_i(priv_lvl) and access_ctrl_i(access_ctrl)
s_valid_rule assigned '1
connectivity_map_o[i][j] = access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i])

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: axi_node internals and complete transaction denial behavior are not visible.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Connectivity is derived from access_ctrl_i and priv_lvl_i, but the visible mapping includes a special-case alias granting j==6 from access_ctrl_i[i][7], and uses a global privilege signal from the processor.
inspection_records.notes: This combines alias and privilege-source concerns; more specific records below isolate them.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI requester indexed by i
evidence_slots.object: PLIC subordinate index 6 and CLINT subordinate index 7 connectivity policy
evidence_slots.expected_guard: special-case permission aliasing between CLINT and PLIC must be intentional and must not grant PLIC access when only CLINT is authorized unless both share the same policy
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: connectivity_mapping ORs access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i] into connectivity_map_o[i][6]. ariane_soc_pkg enumerates PLIC=6 and CLINT=7.
evidence_slots.state_condition: For any requester i and privilege priv_lvl_i, if access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]=1 and j=6, connectivity_map_o[i][6] becomes 1 regardless of access_ctrl_i[i][6][priv_lvl_i].
evidence_slots.impact: A requester authorized only for CLINT can be routed to PLIC, expanding access to interrupt-controller MMIO policy.

rtl_evidence.detail: PLIC=6, CLINT=7
CLINTBase=64'h0200_0000, PLICBase=64'h0C00_0000
j==6 connectivity can be granted by access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: No evidence was supplied that CLINT and PLIC intentionally share policy or that index mapping differs in this instantiation.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: PLIC index 6 is granted when CLINT index 7 is authorized, creating a visible permission alias between distinct package targets.
inspection_records.notes: This is the strongest fabric candidate because all required evidence for the alias is visible.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI manager/requester
evidence_slots.operation: decode and route AXI AW/AR transactions
evidence_slots.object: address-decoded protected regions (DebugBase, ROMBase, ROM2Base, AESBase, CLINTBase, PLICBase, DRAMBase)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: address range checks should reject out-of-range or overlapping protected targets and apply consistently to both read and write address channels
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: start_addr_i/end_addr_i are passed into axi_node_intf_wrap and s_valid_rule is assigned '1. Package base and length constants are visible and appear separated for listed regions. AW and AR channels are both wired into axi_node.
evidence_slots.path: axi_node_intf_wrap: start_addr_i/end_addr_i -> s_start_addr/s_end_addr; s_valid_rule='1; axi_node AW/AR routing
evidence_slots.state_condition: Boundary, burst-crossing, and axi_node range-denial behavior are not visible; read/write channel signals are connected to axi_node.
evidence_slots.impact: Cannot determine from visible wrapper alone whether out-of-range, overlapping, or burst-crossing accesses are rejected.

rtl_evidence.detail: s_start_addr/s_end_addr assigned from start_addr_i/end_addr_i
s_valid_rule assigned '1
length and base constants for Debug, ROM, ROM2, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: axi_node internals are not visible; end_addr_i ordering in the truncated harness observation is incomplete; burst crossing behavior is not evidenced.
inspection_records.notes: No candidate violation is asserted solely from s_valid_rule='1 because valid configured regions may be intentional.

evidence_slots.subject: bus requester driving rom2 req_i/we_i
evidence_slots.object: rom2 secure_reg memory contents containing AES/JTAG/access-control material
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM2/secure_reg writes should be blocked or require explicit privileged/authorized write guard; ROM-like protected memory should not be writable merely when req_i && we_i
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2 loads secure_reg from constant mem on reset, then on req_i and we_i writes secure_reg[addr_i index] <= wdata_i. No local guard checks privilege, lock state, authorization, or write-once policy. Harness connects ROM2 as rom2_fuse through ariane_peripherals on master[ROM2].
evidence_slots.path: AXI/peripheral bus -> ariane_peripherals.rom2_fuse/master[ROM2] -> rom2 req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i -> secure_reg update
evidence_slots.state_condition: On reset secure_reg<=mem; after reset, if req_i is high and we_i is high, write occurs.
evidence_slots.impact: A requester that reaches ROM2 can modify key material and access-control policy source values.

rtl_evidence.detail: comment identifies ROM2 as containing keys
constant mem includes access control, JTAG, and AES key-like values
secure_reg<=mem on reset; secure_reg[index]<=wdata_i on req_i && we_i
ariane_peripherals connects rom2_fuse to master[ariane_soc::ROM2], jtag_key, and access_ctrl_reg

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: ariane_peripherals/ROM2 wrapper internals are not visible; upstream access_ctrl may restrict access, but the local ROM2 module has no write guard.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: ROM2 key/secure registers are writable whenever req_i && we_i, with no local privilege, lock, or write-authorization guard.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate is strong at the module boundary; system exploitability depends on upstream routing policy.

evidence_slots.subject: bus requester observing rom2 rdata_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: reads of secure_reg/ROM2 should be gated by request validity and access authorization, and stale raddr_q data should not remain externally observable after unauthorized or idle cycles
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: raddr_q updates only on req_i && !we_i, while rdata_o continuously reflects secure_reg[raddr_q] if in range. There is no local rdata_o valid gating in rom2.
evidence_slots.path: rom2: raddr_q update on read request; continuous rdata_o = secure_reg[raddr_q]
evidence_slots.state_condition: Idle cycles after a read continue to drive last selected secure_reg entry; reset initializes secure_reg but raddr_q reset value is not shown.
evidence_slots.impact: Potential stale data exposure exists at the module output, but external observation depends on bus response valid/ready and upstream authorization.

rtl_evidence.detail: raddr_q updates on req_i and !we_i
rdata_o continuously assigned from secure_reg[raddr_q] or zero

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: No visible evidence for axi2mem/ariane_peripherals response valid gating or whether rdata_o is externally sampled only during authorized reads.
inspection_records.notes: Module-level data remains driven, but permission violation cannot be closed without bus response behavior.

evidence_slots.subject: bus requester writing ROM2
evidence_slots.object: secure_reg array and adjacent/undefined memory behavior
evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes should be range-checked against RomSize or upstream address decoder must guarantee addr_i indexes are within secure_reg bounds
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The write index uses addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3] without a visible local comparison against RomSize. Because the index width is $clog2(RomSize) for RomSize=4, the sliced index can only represent 0..3 in the visible RTL.
evidence_slots.path: rom2: secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i on req_i && we_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: For RomSize=4, index width is 2 bits; req_i && we_i triggers the write.
evidence_slots.impact: No local explicit bounds check is present, but the truncated index width appears to prevent an index >= RomSize for the current RomSize value.

rtl_evidence.detail: localparam RomSize=4
raddr_q width is [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0]
write index uses addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Upstream decoder guarantee and synthesis semantics for parameter changes are not evidenced.
inspection_records.notes: For the current parameter value, this is not closed as a violation despite missing explicit compare.

evidence_slots.subject: software/bus/debug requester configuring access_ctrl_reg
evidence_slots.expected_guard: software-visible or externally driven access_ctrl_reg updates must be privileged/locked and must not allow unprivileged code to change access policy
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: access_ctrl_reg is a harness signal connected as an output of ariane_peripherals, then sliced into access_ctrl and passed to axi_node_intf_wrap. ROM2 mem comments identify entries for access-control master policy, and rom2 permits writes to secure_reg without a local guard.
evidence_slots.path: rom2/ariane_peripherals access_ctrl_reg -> harness access_ctrl[i][j] -> axi_node connectivity policy
evidence_slots.state_condition: access_ctrl_reg reset/default derives from ROM2/fuse path; updates or write sites inside ariane_peripherals are not visible; connectivity uses current access_ctrl combinationally.
evidence_slots.impact: If a requester can write the ROM2/fuse-backed policy registers, it can alter the matrix used to authorize future bus routing.

rtl_evidence.detail: declares access_ctrl_reg
access_ctrl derived from access_ctrl_reg slices
priv_lvl and access_ctrl passed to xbar
ariane_peripherals connects rom2_fuse, jtag_key, and access_ctrl_reg
mem comments identify access control entries; secure_reg write occurs on req_i && we_i

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: ariane_peripherals internal write/update guard for access_ctrl_reg is not visible.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: access_ctrl_reg is sourced from ariane_peripherals/ROM2 fuse outputs and directly controls bus connectivity; visible policy update/write protection is not shown, while ROM2 secure_reg containing access-control words is locally writable.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate relies on the visible unguarded ROM2 write plus access_ctrl_reg use path; exact update path is partly outside scope.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI requesters including processor and debug master
evidence_slots.operation: authorize AXI read/write based on privilege
evidence_slots.expected_guard: privilege level used for bus access control must correspond to the requesting transaction/master and be stable for the transaction, not a global value that can mismatch debug/other masters or change mid-transfer
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: NB_SUBORDINATE=2 creates two requester/slave ports. dm_top AXI master is connected to slave[1], ariane core to slave[0]. The xbar receives only priv_lvl=priv_lvl_processor from the core and passes that single value to connectivity_mapping for all requester indices.
evidence_slots.path: ariane.priv_lvl_o -> priv_lvl_processor -> priv_lvl -> axi_node_intf_wrap.priv_lvl_i -> connectivity_mapping access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] for all i
evidence_slots.state_condition: Multiple requester ports exist; no per-requester privilege or latching is visible; privilege changes during outstanding transactions are not addressed in visible wrapper.
evidence_slots.impact: Debug-master transactions can be authorized using the processor's current privilege, and outstanding transactions may see a changed global privilege rather than the privilege at issue time.

rtl_evidence.detail: NB_SUBORDINATE=2
dm_top AXI master connects to slave[1]
ariane AXI master connects to slave[0]
priv_lvl assigned from priv_lvl_processor
ariane outputs priv_lvl_processor
same priv_lvl_i indexes access_ctrl for every requester i,j

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: No evidence of per-transaction privilege latching inside axi_node; no separate debug privilege source is visible.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: A single global processor privilege signal is used for connectivity decisions shared by multiple AXI requester ports, including the processor and debug module.
inspection_records.notes: This is a visible design-pattern violation for multi-master authorization.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI requester with prot/user metadata
evidence_slots.object: AXI security/privilege attributes and downstream access decisions
evidence_slots.expected_guard: security attributes such as AXI prot/user must be preserved or explicitly checked; downstream protected targets should not rely on attributes that are dropped, overwritten, or inconsistent across channels
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: axi_node_intf_wrap propagates aw_prot/ar_prot and aw_user/ar_user/w_user/r_user/b_user between interface arrays and axi_node signals. The DRAM bridge path maps prot fields through aw_chan/ar_chan and user fields are assigned directly from master[DRAM] to dram. Permission decisions in the visible wrapper use priv_lvl/access_ctrl rather than prot/user.
evidence_slots.path: AXI metadata propagation through axi_node_intf_wrap and DRAM bridge assignments
evidence_slots.state_condition: Read and write prot/user assignments are visible for wrapper and DRAM bridge; downstream target use of attributes is not visible.
evidence_slots.impact: Cannot determine whether protected targets rely on dropped/stale attributes; visible access control appears separate from prot/user.

rtl_evidence.detail: master aw_prot/aw_user and ar_prot/ar_user assigned from internal signals
slave aw_prot/aw_user, ar_prot/ar_user, w_user captured into internal signals
DRAM bridge propagates prot through aw/ar channels and assigns aw_user/ar_user from master[DRAM]

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Downstream target attribute checks and bridge internals are not visible.
inspection_records.notes: No concrete missing-attribute permission bypass is closed in the visible evidence.

tool_observations.content:
1:  /*
2:  * ROM2: Which have all the keys.
3:  */
4: 
5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48: 
49: 
50: endmodule

409: module connectivity_mapping #(
410:     parameter NB_MANAGER      = 4,
411:     parameter NB_SUBORDINATE       = 4,
412:     parameter NB_PRIV_LVL    = 4,   
413:     parameter PRIV_LVL_WIDTH = 4   
414:   )(
415:     input logic [PRIV_LVL_WIDTH-1:0] 	       priv_lvl_i,   
416:     input logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0][NB_PRIV_LVL-1:0] access_ctrl_i,  
417:     
418:     output logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0]  connectivity_map_o
419:   ); 
420: 
421:     
422:   genvar i,j ; 
423: 
424:   generate
425:     
426:     for (i=0; i<NB_SUBORDINATE; i++)
427:     begin
428:       	for (j=0; j<NB_MANAGER; j++)
429:       	begin
430: 	  assign connectivity_map_o[i][j] = access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]) ; 
431:       	end  		    		      		
432:     end
433: 
434:   endgenerate 
435: 
436: endmodule
